channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Black denomination upholds stance against same-sex marriage. A gay pastor in its ranks seeks change
item.description: The African Methodist Episcopal Church, one of largest Black Protestant denominations in the world, has been wrestling with whether to change a policy that threatens to punish any of its clergy who recognize same-sex marriages.
item.dc:creator: Darren Sands, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bill Clinton says he’s open to talking to Biden about preemptive pardon for Hillary Clinton
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton said he is open to having a conversation with President Joe Biden about a potential preemptive pardon for his wife, former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump named Time's Person of the Year
item.description: About six months ago, Donald Trump was sitting in a courtroom in lower Manhattan listening to a jury make him the first former president convicted of a crime.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden commutes roughly 1,500 sentences and pardons 39 people in biggest single-day act of clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is commuting the sentences of roughly 1,500 people who were released from prison and placed on home confinement during the coronavirus pandemic and is pardoning 39 Americans convicted of nonviolent crimes.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: American who says he crossed into Syria by foot is free after 7 months
item.description: An American who turned up in Syria on Thursday says he was detained after crossing into the country by foot on a Christian pilgrimage seven months ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Drones' reign of terror gripping Americans reaches fever pitch and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turning dusty attic treasures into cash can yield millions for some and disappointment for others
item.description: THOMASTON, Maine (AP) — Kaja Veilleux has been hunting New England attic treasures for more than 50 years.
item.dc:creator: NICK PERRY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Southern California forecast of cool temps, calm winds to help firefighters battle Malibu blaze
item.description: Cooler temperatures, calmer winds and a chance of rainfall in Southern California this week are forecast to help firefighters as they battle a wind-driven wildfire.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Weber, Jae C. Hong And Gabriela Aoun Angueira, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Secretly recorded videos are backbone of corruption trial for longest-serving legislative leader
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Hours of secretly recorded videos and phone calls have offered a rare glimpse into how the longest-serving legislative leader in American history operated behind closed doors.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 men go free after their convictions were overturned in a case involving a discredited detective
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Kan. (AP) — Two men were freed Wednesday after their convictions were overturned in a 2009 double homicide whose investigation was overseen by a discredited white Kansas City, Kansas, police detective.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Homeland Security shares new details of mysterious drone flights over New Jersey
item.description: The large mysterious drones reported flying over parts of New Jersey in recent weeks appear to avoid detection by traditional methods such as helicopter and radio, according to a state lawmaker briefed Wednesday by the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: East Coast storm makes a mess at ski resorts as strong winds cause power outages
item.description: A storm that swept up the East Coast has caused some misery for New England ski resort operators, but drier and cooler weather is in the forecast for the next few days.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle And David Sharp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Dakota regulators consider underground carbon dioxide storage permits for Midwest pipeline
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — A North Dakota panel will consider Thursday whether to approve permits for underground storage of hundreds of millions of metric tons of carbon dioxide that a proposed pipeline would carry from ethanol plants throughout the Midwest.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Atmospheric river brings weather whiplash to East Coast as bomb cyclone develops
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The U.S. East Coast began a whiplash-inducing stretch of weather Wednesday with a deluge of rain, rapid snowmelt and powerful gusts, creating dangerous conditions, due in part to an atmospheric river and developing bomb cyclone.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FIFA confirms Saudi Arabia as 2034 World Cup host despite human rights concerns
item.description: ZURICH (AP) — Saudi Arabia was officially confirmed Wednesday by FIFA as host of the 2034 World Cup in men’s soccer, giving the oil-rich kingdom its biggest prize yet for massive spending on global sports driven by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Lawmakers edge closer to deal on government funding
item.description: Lawmakers say they are edging closer to a deal to keep the government's lights on in nine days but are still trying to work out differences over disaster aid and potential add-ons to the bill. There’s some optimism in the Capitol that bill text could arrive as soon as this weekend as the Dec.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing could return to New York on a governor's warrant
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The suspect in the killing of the UnitedHealthcare CEO plans to fight extradition to New York to face murder charges, but officials hope to get him back with what’s called a governor’s warrant. The process could happen quickly or take more than a month. New York Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House approves mammoth annual defense policy bill
item.description: The House passed the annual defense policy bill Wednesday, sending the mammoth measure to the Senate for consideration ahead of Congress’s end-of-month deadline.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Increased Christianity in schools opens the door to Satanic Temple education programs
item.description: The Satanic Temple does not seek to convert students toward Satan but wants to be a bulwark against increased religious education.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Immigration surge under Biden is largest in US history: NYT
item.description: According to the Times, the 2021 to 2023 arrival rate is the quickest in comparison to the rest of U.S. history, being the swiftest surge in nearly 175 years.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Fentanyl found inside dead dolphins in Gulf of Mexico
item.description: Drug smugglers using open waterways to move products may be affecting the health of sea life in the Gulf of Mexico
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Albertsons gives up on Kroger merger, sues the grocery chain
item.description: Kroger and Albertsons in 2022 proposed what would be the largest grocery store merger in U.S. history. The companies said a merger would help them better compete with big retailers like Walmart, Costco and Amazon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bernie Sanders drops major hint about political future
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.), one of the Senate’s most prominent progressives, says he likely won’t run again for the Senate after his fourth term expires in 2030. Asked in an interview with Politico whether this would be his last term in Congress, Sanders replied: “I’m 83 now.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KTLA 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione's fingerprints match water bottle found near shooting: sources
item.description: Fingerprints taken from Luigi Mangione in Pennsylvania matched those on a water bottle found near the scene of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's fatal shooting, sources said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KRON4

item.title: Violent Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua spreads to one of America’s most remote states
item.description: The Venezuelan prison gang Tren de Aragua has now spread to one of the most remote states in America, after an alleged member was arrested for felony theft last month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Malibu wildfire prompts evacuation orders and warnings for 20,000, including Dick Van Dyke and Cher
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — Weather conditions were forecast to improve in Southern California, aiding firefighters in their battle against a wind-driven wildfire that has forced up to 20,000 people, including Cher, Dick Van Dyke and his wife, from their homes in Malibu.
item.dc:creator: JAE C. HONG, ERIC THAYER and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The burial site of the people Andrew Jackson enslaved was lost. The Hermitage says it is found
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — At least 26 enslaved people died on the Tennessee plantation of President Andrew Jackson between 1804 and the end of the Civil War in 1865. Where they were laid to rest is knowledge that had been lost to time.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kayaker who faked his death, fled the US charged after returning to Wisconsin
item.description: Police had previously said they believed Borgwardt planned his Aug. 12 disappearance and tried to conceal his efforts to mislead investigations.
item.dc:creator: Indiana Schilz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Luigi Mangione was reported missing 2 weeks before CEO slain: police
item.description: The parents of Luigi Mangione, the man suspected of shooting and killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, told law enforcement that they hadn't heard from their son in months.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Nicole Johnson, ABC 4

item.title: Sanders says this will likely be his last term
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.), one of the Senate’s most prominent progressives, says he likely won’t run again for the Senate after his fourth term expires in 2030.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Customer recalls moment Luigi Mangione spotted in Pennsylvania McDonald's
item.description: New details are emerging about what happened at the Altoona, Pennsylvania McDonald's where police arrested Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Trump trolls Trudeau as 'governor' of Canada
item.description: President-elect Trump mocked Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, calling him the "governor" of the "Great State of Canada.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: US wildlife officials plan to label monarch butterflies as threatened species
item.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service plans to add the butterfly to the threatened species list by the end of next year following an extensive public comment period.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas judge dismisses infamous 'Black Widow' murder case after 27 years
item.description: The judge dismissed murder charges against Margaret Rudin, the woman dubbed "The Black Widow" who a jury convicted of killing her husband more than two decades ago.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KRON4

item.title: Five California Lottery players become millionaires from Scratcher games
item.description: Five California Lottery players are the state’s newest millionaires after winning a combined total of $17 million from Scratcher games, lottery officials announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4
